_provenance.stage: Stage 3 — Fact Verification (ChatGPT, browser, on v3)
_provenance.note: Principal fact-check. Critical corrections: (1) SUARAM claim INCORRECT — the April 2025 launch was the 2024 Human Rights Report and reported a five-year high in CHARGES UNDER THE SEDITION ACT FOR 2024 not 2025; SUARAM's 2025 overview launched 5 Dec 2025, not 'a month before' Zahid's 10 May 2026 speech. (2) FC ruling: 'political speech excluded; single post not enough' overreads the press summary — press summary actually says political views causing anger don't constitute harm, but communications attacking constitutional role of monarchy WITH INTENT can fall within s.233. (3) 'Three statutes criminalise this' is overbroad — the statutes have distinct elements. (4) 'Fourteen years later it is still in cabinet draft' conflates the 2012 Najib Sedition replacement promise with the April 2026 SKRN Commission Bill — they may not be the same instrument; also the period is ~13y10m, not a full 14 years. (5) CIJ 36/19 Sedition-specific figure: CIJ primary press release verifies 233/189 broader basket but not 36/19; secondary sources (FMT, Daily Express, The Star) do. (6) RM500K and 'grossly offensive' VERIFIED against Act A1743 directly.

key_corrections.claim: SUARAM had reported a month earlier that 2025 Sedition Act charges hit a five-year high.
key_corrections.primary_source: SUARAM, Launch of Malaysia Human Rights Report 2024, 10 Apr 2025: https://www.suaram.net/post/launch-of-suaram-s-malaysia-human-rights-report-2024 ; SUARAM, Launch of Malaysia Human Rights Report Overview 2025, 5 Dec 2025: https://www.suaram.net/post/launch-of-suaram-s-malaysia-human-rights-report-overview-2025
key_corrections.correct_value: SUARAM's April 2025 launch covered the 2024 Human Rights Report (5-year high reported was for 2024, not 2025). SUARAM's 2025 overview launched 5 Dec 2025. Neither is 'a month before' Zahid's 10 May 2026 speech.

key_corrections.claim: Either way, political speech is excluded; a single post is not enough.
key_corrections.primary_source: Federal Court press summary, Government of Malaysia v Heidy Quah [2026] MLJU 384, 6 Feb 2026
key_corrections.correct_value: The press summary says political views and controversial issues that merely cause discontent or anger do not constitute harm and are protected free speech. It also says communications attacking the constitutional role of the monarchy WITH INTENT to annoy, abuse, threaten or harass CAN fall within s.233. The blanket 'political speech excluded' is overread.

key_corrections.claim: Three statutes criminalise this: Sedition Act, Penal Code 298A, and CMA s.233.
key_corrections.primary_source: Sedition Act 1948 s.3; Penal Code 298A; CMA s.233 (Act A1743)
key_corrections.correct_value: The statutes have distinct elements: sedition (as defined), religious disharmony (PC 298A), and specified improper network communications (CMA s.233). They do not uniformly criminalise 'insult' as a single category. Safer: 'At least three existing statutes may be invoked depending on the facts.'

key_corrections.claim: Fourteen years later, it is still in cabinet draft.
key_corrections.primary_source: Bernama 11 Jul 2012 (Najib announcement); Ministry of National Unity statement 7 Apr 2026: https://dmedia.penerangan.gov.my/upload/km/07042026.458631609.pdf
key_corrections.correct_value: The 2012 promise was specifically about replacing the Sedition Act with a National Harmony Act. The April 2026 cabinet review is on a National Harmony and Reconciliation Commission (SKRN) Bill. These may not be the same legal instrument. Period is ~13y10m, not full 14 years. Safer: 'Nearly fourteen years after a 2012 promise to replace the Sedition Act, a related harmony and reconciliation bill is still being studied by cabinet.'

key_corrections.claim: Sedition Act charges jumped 84% in 2025 — 36 cases, up from 19 in 2024.
key_corrections.primary_source: CIJ primary press statement 10 Dec 2025 verifies 233/189 broader basket and 23.28% increase. Secondary citations (FMT, Daily Express, The Star focus) cite 36/19 from CIJ FoE Report 2025 itself, which was not located in the public-search pass.
key_corrections.correct_value: Keep the figures but attribute as 'CIJ said' rather than 'CIJ Report logged'. Soft attribution.

key_corrections.claim: the highest since the 2015 online-speech amendments
key_corrections.correct_value: Remove or soften unless a primary longitudinal table from CIJ, SUARAM, Parliament, PDRM or AGC is cited.

key_corrections.primary_source: Demographic split found in The Star 15 Dec 2025 citing CIJ; not located in CIJ primary press release.
key_corrections.correct_value: Attribute to The Star reporting CIJ, or drop.

verified: Zahid speech 10 May 2026, Musyawarah Nasional, 'firm, fair, wise' framing

Communications and Multimedia (Amendment) Act 2025 (Act A1743): gazetted 7 Feb 2025, in force 11 Feb 2025, substitutes 'grossly offensive' for 'offensive', raises s.233 max to RM500,000, jail 2 years, RM5,000/day continuing penalty; RM1M ceiling on commercial-purpose obscene content
Federal Court 6 Feb 2026 ruling, [2026] MLJU 384, reinstated 'offensive' and 'annoy' while reading the provision down (intent/harm)
The Heidy Quah case turned on pre-amendment text; current law says 'grossly offensive'
Sedition Act s.3(1)(f) references Article 181 (Rulers) — precise wording: protects matters established by Part III or Articles 152, 153 or 181
Najib 2012 promise to replace Sedition Act with National Harmony Act (11 Jul 2012)
overall_assessment: Analysis is directionally strong on the Zahid speech, the legal stack, the 2025 CMA amendment and the broad fact that the Federal Court reinstated the pre-amendment 'offensive/annoy' wording while reading the provision narrowly. NOT publication-grade until: fix the SUARAM timing/year claim; downgrade or source the CIJ Sedition-specific figures; avoid categorical 'political speech excluded' / 'single post never enough'; avoid conflating the 2012 Sedition replacement promise with the April 2026 SKRN commission bill.
